channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Behind the noise of an ‘Iran deal’, Palestine continues to burn
item.description: Israel's war on the Palestinians never stopped; the world just stopped watching.

item.title: Mexico beat South Korea 1-0, become first team to reach World Cup knockouts
item.description: Luis Romo's 50th-minute goal sent Mexico into the round of 32, which they will play on home soil, too.

item.title: Norway MP’s join viral World Cup ‘Viking Row’ trend
item.description: Norwegian lawmakers paused a parliamentary session on Thursday to perform the team's trademark “Viking Row” celebration.

item.title: Ukraine urges EU to pressure Russia to end war before winter
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy say his country will need help if the war with Russia continues into the winter.

item.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon in sudden clash surge with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel strikes southern Lebanon in sudden clash surge with Hezbollah

item.title: Andy Burnham wins key UK by-election, paving way to challenge Keir Starmer
item.description: Greater Manchester mayor's victory in Makerfield clears path for bid to unseat Starmer as prime minister.

item.title: Canada vs Qatar World Cup: 3 goals for David, 2 red cards, 1 injured Kone
item.description: BC Place was a battlefield as Kone was stretchered off, Qatar got two red cards, and both teams brawled after full-time.

item.title: Obama opens presidential centre with call for unity
item.description: Former US President Barack Obama opened his presidential centre in Chicago, using the occasion to call for unity.

item.title: Paint peeling after $14 million memorial pool renovation
item.description: Paint is peeling and algae is blooming less than two weeks after the $14 million renovation of DC’s Reflecting Pool.

item.title: EU won’t lift key Iran sanctions until formal nuclear deal reached
item.description: EU won't lift key Iran sanctions until formal nuclear deal reached

item.title: Zimbabwe bill to scrap presidential elections sparks backlash
item.description: Supporters call the bill a governance reform, while opponents see a turning point for Zimbabwe's political future.

item.title: Iran war live: JD Vance delays trip to Switzerland for talks with Tehran
item.description: US vice president says 60-day negotiating period between Washington and Tehran has begun after deal to end war signed.

item.title: Vote counting begins in high-stakes UK by-election
item.description: Ballots are being counted in the UK’s closely watched Makerfield by-election.

item.title: US judge orders release of Palestinian rights advocate detained by ICE
item.description: Mosque leader says he was arrested and detained by immigration authorities for his Palestine advocacy.

item.title: Switzerland beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1, top Group B in World Cup
item.description: All five goals were scored in the final 30 minutes as the match in Los Angeles erupted to life in the closing stages.

item.title: Mamdani gives New York Knicks keys to city after NBA title
item.description: The New York Knicks celebrated their first NBA title in 53 years with a frenzied ticker-tape parade through Manhattan.

item.title: Are prices really dropping in the US, as Trump claims?
item.description: Donald Trump boasts on social media that since signing of MoU for peace deal, oil is flowing and prices are dropping.

item.title: Cuba’s Communist Party approves opening economy in unprecedented move
item.description: The emergency economic package comes amid an ongoing US pressure campaign that has left the island nation reeling.

item.title: Myanmar military airstrike kills seven civilians in Rakhine state
item.description: A Myanmar military airstrike on the Rakhine state town of Kyauktaw killed at least seven civilians.

item.title: Brazil executes warrant against Lula ally in widening financial scandal
item.description: Left-wing Senator Jaques Wagner was the latest prominent figure to be swept up in investigations into Banco Master.

item.title: Conjoined twins separated after surgery in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Filipino conjoined twins Olivia and Gianna were successfully separated during a six-hour surgery in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Neymar will not play in Brazil’s World Cup match against Haiti due to injury
item.description: Brazil's record goal-scorer continues to struggle with injuries after being named in their World Cup 2026 squad.

item.title: Ivory Coast’s Elye Wahi granted Canadian visa hours after denial
item.description: The forward will now be able to join his team in Toronto when they take on Germany on Saturday.

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel must protect relationship with US
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pressed for the ‘preservation’ of Israel’s relationship with the US.

item.title: Pro-Israel hawks in US criticise Iran MoU but avoid clashing with Trump
item.description: Analysts warn that pro-Israel groups and politicians in Washington will likely push to derail diplomacy with Tehran.
